tools.name: find_providers
tools.description: Find a venue in the Aurinia network (doctor, hostel, spa). Optional: query (e.g. 'vertebrologist'), city, type. Returns a providers array; use a provider_id from it for get_availability / book.

tools.name: get_provider_info
tools.description: Details for one venue by provider_id: id, type (doctor/hostel/spa), name, city, address. Read-only, no auth. Use it to confirm a venue before get_availability / book.

tools.name: get_availability
tools.description: Availability and price at a venue. provider_id is required. Use EXACTLY these param names: doctor — date (YYYY-MM-DD); hostel — check_in, check_out, guests (with underscores: check_in/check_out, NOT 'checkin'/'checkout'; guests is an integer). There is no room_type param — a dorm bed is the default.

tools.name: book
tools.description: REAL action: book at a network venue on the user's behalf. provider_id + item (the venue interprets it). Doctor: item={date,time}. Hostel: item={check_in,check_out,guests,promo_code?} — do NOT ask for name/contact: identity comes from a one-time Telegram consent. Spa: item={date,time,procedure}. Hos

tools.name: reschedule
tools.description: REAL action: reschedule a booking at a network venue. provider_id + item (doctor: {date, from, to} — SAME day, ATOMIC: if the target slot was just taken, the user KEEPS their current slot — never loses the held slot; that guarantee is the point). For a DIFFERENT day just book again (cross-day needs

tools.name: cancel
tools.description: REAL action: cancel a booking at a network venue IMMEDIATELY — no confirmation step. If the user said cancel, cancel; do NOT re-ask (repeated confirmations frustrate users — act decisively). provider_id + item {booking_id} — the booking id from list_current_bookings. Do NOT ask for consent up front

tools.name: list_current_bookings
tools.description: The user's current/upcoming bookings at a network venue (by date, nearest first). Each has a status field (e.g. booked/confirmed/cancelled) — distinguish active vs cancelled. provider_id optional (without it — the first venue). Requires authorization.

tools.name: list_past_bookings
tools.description: The user's past bookings at a network venue (by date, most recent first). Each has a status field. provider_id optional; limit (default 20)/offset for pagination. Requires authorization.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "book" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "book" stacks urgency/override words (immediately, always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "list_current_bookings" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "find_providers" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Знайти заклад у мережі Aurinia (лікар, хостел, спа). query (напр. 'вертебролог'), city, type — опційно." → after: "Find a venue in the Aurinia network (doctor, hostel, spa). Optional: query (e.g. 'vertebrologist'), city, type. Returns a providers array; use a provider_id from it for get_availability / book."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

Each has a status field (e.g. booked/confirmed/cancelled) — distinguish active vs cancelled. provider_id optional (without it — the first venue). Requires authorization."
